evals.prompt: When should I use @State vs @Binding in SwiftUI?
evals.expected_output: Use @State for data owned by the view. Use @Binding for data passed down from a parent view to a child.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between @StateObject and @ObservedObject?
evals.expected_output: Use @StateObject when the view is creating the instance. Use @ObservedObject when receiving an instance from an external source.

evals.prompt: How do I share data across a view hierarchy in SwiftUI?
evals.expected_output: Use @Environment or @EnvironmentObject to inject data into the view's hierarchy. Example: parent.environmentObject(user).

assertions.value: inject data into the view's hierarchy

evals.prompt: How do I use LazyVStack for performance?
evals.expected_output: Use LazyVStack when displaying a large number of views in a scrolling container to load them only as they appear. Use LazyHGrid for grid performance.

assertions.value: load them only as they appear

should_not_trigger: How do I implement SSL pinning?
How do I set up Core Data?
